terminology.notes: "parameter" (def-time) vs "argument" (call-time) is defined once in 3.1 (5.2) and used consistently thereafter; neither 3.2 nor 3.3 redefines it. "scope" (local/global) is defined only in 3.1 and not contradicted elsewhere. "namespace"/"namespacing" is introduced only in 3.3 (5.1, module dot-notation) and does not collide with 3.1's variable-scope terminology since they name distinct concepts and 3.1 never uses the word "namespace". "higher-order function", "decorator", "generator", "lazy evaluation" are each defined once (3.2) and not redefined elsewhere. No synonym drift or conflicting definitions of a shared term found.

prereq_ordering.notes: 3.1 prerequisites (1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.2) are all earlier-in-sequence topics. 3.2 prerequisites (3.1, 2.2, 1.3, 1.2) reference only 3.1 (earlier position in same module) and earlier weeks; 3.2 explicitly reuses *args/**kwargs vocabulary from 3.1 rather than treating it as new. 3.3 prerequisites (3.1, 2.2, 1.1) are likewise all earlier. No topic assumes a concept from a later topic in the module (3.1 never uses lambdas/decorators/generators/modules; 3.2 never uses modules/pip/venv; 3.3 never uses lambdas/decorators).

duplication.notes: 3.2's decorator section (5.3) references the *args/**kwargs collecting pattern from 3.1 by name ("uses exactly the *args/**kwargs collecting pattern from 3.1") without re-explaining what * and ** do mechanically — correct reuse, not re-teaching. Recursion (3.1) is not duplicated in 3.2 or 3.3. No two topics' Core Concepts sections substantively overlap; each topic's terminology set is disjoint from the others'.

learning_arc.notes: narrative_seed_by_week[3] = 'Functions, parameters, scope, recursion; lambdas, decorators, generators; modules & imports; pip, venv, Poetry, Pytest'. All facets are covered: functions/parameters/scope/recursion -> 3.1; lambdas/decorators/generators -> 3.2; modules & imports/pip/venv/Poetry/Pytest -> 3.3. No uncovered facet.

lab_alignment.notes: function with defaults -> 3.1 Hands-On Exercise (greet(name, greeting="Hello")). recursion (factorial) -> 3.1 Hands-On Exercise (factorial(n)). lambda sort -> 3.2 Hands-On Exercise (sorted(words, key=lambda w: len(w))). @timer decorator -> 3.2 Hands-On Exercise + Implementation/Worked Example (matches step-by-step build). stdlib imports -> 3.3 Hands-On Exercise (dice.py using random.randint). venv + pip + Poetry + Pytest -> 3.3 Implementation (poetry new/add/run walkthrough) + Hands-On Exercise (venv commands sketch, test_dice.py with assert); these are appropriately conceptual/offline per source_outline's explicit '(concept)' / '(concept + offline practice)' tags. One partial gap noted below re: the Fibonacci-generator lab.

findings.finding: lab_activity_seed_by_week[3] names 'Fibonacci generator' as the generator lab. 3.2's Core Concepts (5.4), diagram_intent, and Worked/Key-Takeaways content all teach the Fibonacci generator in depth, but the topic's own Hands-On Exercise (section 9) substitutes a different, simpler generator (`countdown(n)`) instead of asking the learner to write/run the Fibonacci generator themselves. Recommend either swapping the exercise to build on `fibonacci()` directly, or adding a second short Fibonacci-specific exercise step so the seeded lab activity has a matching hands-on artifact rather than only a taught concept.

Total week-3 expected_delivery_minutes across the three topics is 30 (10+10+10) against a 90-minute weekly target in artifact-mix.json's weekly_balance_rule — outside the +/-25% band. This was already flagged individually at HITL#3 per-topic and is properly a course-drift-critic concern, but is directly observable here and is repeated for visibility. Not treated as a module-drift blocking failure.
